1610.00876.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for all oriented paths (Corollary 20, with $\mathrm{mader}_{\delta^+}(P)=|V(P)|-1$), for all in-arborescences (Theorem 23), and for the union of two directed paths from $x$ to $y$ and one from $y$ to $x$ (Theorem 24); also studies an analogue via the dichromatic number parameter.
1610.00876.title: Mader's Conjecture (Conjecture 2)
1610.00876.paper_title: Subdivisions in digraphs of large out-degree or large dichromatic number

2103.07886.paper_contribution: Proves that every locally in-tournament digraph satisfies the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture, deducing it from the structural decomposition theorem (Theorem 2.3).
2103.07886.title: Caccetta-Häggkvist Conjecture
2103.07886.paper_title: Decomposing and colouring some locally semicomplete digraphs

1110.4077.paper_contribution: Proves a special case as Theorem 2.8: for any graph $G$ of treewidth at most $k$ with $\Delta(G) \geq (k+2)^{2k+2}$, $\mathrm{ch}_T(G) = \chi_T(G) = \Delta(G)+1$, yielding a stronger form of the conjecture for this class.
1110.4077.title: Total Colouring Conjecture
1110.4077.paper_title: The Parameterised Complexity of List Problems on Graphs of Bounded Treewidth

1609.05458.paper_contribution: Constructs $r$-partite $r$-uniform intersecting hypergraphs with cover number at least $r-4$ for all sufficiently large $r$, showing that Ryser's Conjecture is close to best possible for every value of $r$.
1609.05458.title: Ryser's Conjecture
1609.05458.paper_title: A note on intersecting hypergraphs with large cover number

1609.06257.paper_contribution: Proves Gallai's conjecture for all connected graphs $G$ with $\Delta(G) \leq 5$, by showing that any smallest counterexample cannot contain any of five reducible configurations, forcing $G_E$ to be a forest and applying Pyber's earlier theorem.
1609.06257.title: Conjecture 1.1 (Gallai's Path Decomposition Conjecture)
1609.06257.paper_title: Gallai's path decomposition conjecture for graphs of small maximum degree

1710.03745.paper_contribution: Proves (Theorem 1.1) that every $n$-vertex graph with VC-dimension at most $d$ contains a clique or an independent set of size at least $e^{(\log n)^{1-o(1)}}$, nearly matching the conjectured polynomial lower bound $n^{\varepsilon(\mathcal{P})}$ and improving the general lower bound $e^{\varepsilon\sqrt{\log n}}$ of Erdős and Hajnal for all hereditary properties.
1710.03745.title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture
1710.03745.paper_title: Erdos-Hajnal conjecture for graphs with bounded VC-dimension

1710.06680.paper_contribution: Applies the main result (Theorem 1.1, that every $t$-dominating graph has bounded local difference from a $0$-dominating graph) to the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture; the details are in the final section of the paper.
1710.06680.title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture

1810.00811.paper_contribution: Proves that for every caterpillar subdivision $H$ there exists $c > 0$ such that every graph $G$ that is both $H$-free and $\bar{H}$-free satisfies $\max(\omega(G), \alpha(G)) \geq |V(G)|^c$, extending results of Bousquet–Lagoutte–Thomassé (paths) and Choromanski–Falik–Liebenau–Patel–Pilipczuk (hooks).
1810.00811.title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture

1912.01570.paper_contribution: Proves Jones' Conjecture for all subcubic planar multigraphs, i.e., establishes $\mathrm{fvs}(G) \leq 2 \cdot \mathrm{cp}(G)$ when the maximum degree is at most 3.
1912.01570.title: Jones' Conjecture

1912.02342.paper_contribution: Not proved here; mentioned in the concluding remarks as an important open problem motivating future work on graphs with bounded VC-dimension.
1912.02342.title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture
1912.02342.paper_title: Bounded VC-dimension implies the Schur-Erdos conjecture

2102.04994.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for $H = C_5$: every $C_5$-free graph $G$ satisfies $\max(\alpha(G), \omega(G)) \geq |G|^\tau$ for some absolute constant $\tau > 0$.
2102.04994.title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture

2102.08679.paper_contribution: Proves that the degree sequence of any graph with average degree at most $d$ can be reconstructed from any deck missing at most $\frac{n}{10^4 d^3}$ cards; in particular, for graphs embeddable on a fixed surface (e.g., planar graphs), the degree sequence is reconstructible even when a linear number of cards are missing.
2102.08679.title: Reconstruction Conjecture
2102.08679.paper_title: Reconstructing the degree sequence of a sparse graph from a partial deck

2110.08870.paper_contribution: Proves Gallai's conjecture for the entire class of planar graphs (Theorem 1.1): every connected planar graph on $n$ vertices can be decomposed into $\lceil n/2 \rceil$ paths.
2110.08870.title: Gallai's Conjecture
2110.08870.paper_title: Gallai's path decomposition in planar graphs

2206.10733.paper_contribution: Does not prove new bounds for the conjecture directly; instead uses existing approximations for the triangle case as a tool to establish improved triangularity bounds for Aharoni's rainbow generalization, and notes that improvements to those approximations would yield improvements to Theorem 3.
2206.10733.title: Caccetta-Häggkvist Conjecture
2206.10733.paper_title: Improved bounds for the triangle case of Aharoni's rainbow generalization of the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture

2302.08182.paper_contribution: Proves a polynomial-time algorithm running in $n^{O(t^{5})}$ for MIS in graphs excluding $K_{1}+tK_{2}$ as an induced minor, settling Question 1 for this infinite family of planar graphs.
2302.08182.title: Question 1
2302.08182.paper_title: Maximum Independent Set when excluding an induced minor: $K_1 + tK_2$ and $tC_3 \uplus C_4$

2307.06379.paper_contribution: Proves that $\{H,\overline{J}\}$ has the Erdős-Hajnal property for any two cycles $H,J$, and more generally when $H$ and $J$ are obtained from smaller graphs by subdividing every edge exactly twice (or, for one of them, by subdividing every non-forest edge at least five times); this simultaneously extends the $C_5$ result and resolves the open question from [6] about longer cycles.
2307.06379.title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture
2307.06379.paper_title: Induced subgraph density. III. Cycles and subdivisions

2403.08303.paper_contribution: Proves that the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture is equivalent to both the polynomial Rödl conjecture (Conjecture 2) and the polynomial Nikiforov conjecture (Conjecture 3), extending several previous partial equivalence results.
2403.08303.title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture
2403.08303.paper_title: Equivalence between Erdős-Hajnal and polynomial Rödl and Nikiforov conjectures

2408.04618.paper_contribution: Shows that every connected vertex-transitive graph on $n\geq 3$ vertices contains a cycle (and hence a path) of length at least $\Omega(n^{13/21})$, improving the previous bound of $\Omega(n^{3/5})$ due to DeVos (2023).
2408.04618.title: Lovász Conjecture
2408.04618.paper_title: Longest cycles in vertex-transitive and highly connected graphs

2502.04177.paper_contribution: Introduces depth-$r$ bramble number, tangle number, linkedness, and well-linkedness, proves they are pairwise polynomially related and all polynomially bounded in $r$ for classes with polynomial expansion, providing potential new tools toward resolving the question positively.
2502.04177.title: Question 1

2505.08634.paper_contribution: Shows that every connected vertex-transitive graph of order $n$ contains a path of length $\Omega(n^{9/14})$, improving the previous best bound of $\Omega(n^{13/21})$.
2505.08634.title: Lovász Conjecture
2505.08634.paper_title: Small hitting sets for longest paths and cycles

2603.14427.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for the stronger correspondence chromatic number $\chi_{DP}$, showing that for every graph $G$ with $\Delta(G)\geq 3\cdot 10^9$, $\chi_{DP}(G)\leq\max(\omega(G),\Delta(G)-1)$.
2603.14427.title: Borodin-Kostochka Conjecture
2603.14427.paper_title: On Borodin-Kostochka conjecture for correspondence coloring

2502.01451.paper_contribution: Proves a density bound $|E(G)| \leq 3|V(G)| - 5$ for flow-critical graphs with at most one vertex of degree at least 7 (Theorem 1.6), and extends the Lovász et al. 6-edge-connectivity theorem to allow the tip vertex $z$ to have arbitrary degree when another high-degree vertex exists with no small separating cut.
2502.01451.title: 3-Flow Conjecture (Tutte)

2012.03686.paper_contribution: Proves a biclique-exclusion relaxation: every $C_{\geq t}$-free graph containing no $K_{\ell,\ell}$ as a subgraph satisfies $\chi(G) \leq \ell^{f(t)}+1$, giving a polynomial bound in $\ell$ under the additional assumption of no large balanced biclique.
2012.03686.title: Conjecture 1 (Gyárfás–Sumner)
2012.03686.paper_title: Degeneracy of $P_t$-free and $C_{\geq t}$-free graphs with no large complete bipartite subgraphs

2312.15333.paper_contribution: Proves that $P_5$ satisfies the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture (Theorem 1.2), thereby completing the verification of the conjecture for all five-vertex graphs.
2312.15333.title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
2312.15333.paper_title: Induced subgraph density. VII. The five-vertex path

2307.06455.paper_contribution: Proves infinitely many prime graphs satisfy the conjecture: all graphs $H$ whose every non-trivial prime induced subgraph $H'$ has a vertex of degree one and a vertex of degree $|H'|-2$; more generally, proves it whenever $H_1$ and $\overline{H_2}$ are both buildable (every prime induced subgraph with at least three vertices has a vertex of degree one).
2307.06455.title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
2307.06455.paper_title: Induced subgraph density. IV. New graphs with the Erdős-Hajnal property

2110.00278.paper_contribution: Proves the near-polynomial bound $\chi(G) \leq \omega(G)^{\log_2(\omega(G))}$ for $P_5$-free graphs with $\omega(G) \geq 3$, significantly improving the previously best known exponential bound $(5/27)3^{\omega(G)}$ within the Gyárfás-Sumner framework.
2110.00278.title: Gyárfás-Sumner Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
2110.00278.paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number. IV. A near-polynomial bound for excluding the five-vertex path

2312.15572.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for every two-colourable tournament, which in particular completes the verification of the conjecture for all six-vertex tournaments.
2312.15572.title: Tournament Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 6.1)
2312.15572.paper_title: Induced subgraph density. VI. Bounded VC-dimension

1708.09579.paper_contribution: Proves counting analogues in related settings: at least $2^{n/7}$ nowhere-zero $Z_2\times Z_3$-flows in 3-edge-connected graphs ($2^{2(m-n)/9}$ for 2-edge-connected), at least $2^{n/250}$ nowhere-zero $Z_2^2$-flows in 4-edge-connected graphs, and at least $2^{(n-2)/12}$ nowhere-zero $Z_3$-flows in 6-edge-connected graphs. These are exponential counting versions of the existence theorems that are partial results toward Tutte's conjectures, but do not directly resolve them.
1708.09579.title: Tutte's Flow Conjectures (Conjecture 1.2)

2009.09426.paper_contribution: Proves bipartite analogues of the strong Erdős-Hajnal property (Theorems 1.5 and 1.6), establishing that excluding a forest bigraph and its bicomplement forces linear-sized pure pairs in each part, and providing one-sided sparse versions; these results imply bipartite Erdős-Hajnal-type bounds.
2009.09426.title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
2009.09426.paper_title: Pure pairs. IV. Trees in bipartite graphs

1806.00825.paper_contribution: Proves the $r=2$ case of Aharoni's rainbow strengthening of this conjecture (Theorem 5), which implies the $r=2$ case of Caccetta-Häggkvist. Also introduces Conjecture 4, a weaker rainbow properly-coloured-cycle condition shown to imply the full Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture.
1806.00825.title: Caccetta-Häggkvist Conjecture (Conjecture 1)
1806.00825.paper_title: Short rainbow cycles in graphs and matroids
paper_authors: Sebastián González Hermosillo de la Maza

1809.08324.paper_contribution: Shows that the Caccetta-Häggkvist conjecture implies the bipartite analogue Conjecture 1.2; uses CH-approximations to prove 1.2 for $k = 1, 2, 3, 4, 6$, and all $k \geq 224{,}539$.
1809.08324.title: Caccetta-Häggkvist Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
1809.08324.paper_title: Short directed cycles in bipartite digraphs

2201.09115.paper_contribution: Disproves the conjecture in a strong form: for every $\varepsilon > 0$ and $C \geq 1$ there exists $N = N(\varepsilon, C)$ such that for all integers $s, t$ with $N \leq s \leq t \leq Cs$, there exists a graph without a $K_{s,t}$-minor and list chromatic number greater than $(1-\varepsilon)(2s+t)$, substantially exceeding the conjectured bound of $s+t-1$.
2201.09115.title: Woodall's Conjecture C(r,s,ch)
2201.09115.paper_title: Disproof of a Conjecture by Woodall

2005.02896.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for the class of hole-with-hat-free graphs: there exists $\varepsilon > 0$ such that every hole-with-hat-free graph $G$ has a clique or stable set of cardinality at least $|G|^\varepsilon$. This strictly generalises the house-free case (since hole-with-hat-free implies house-free) but does not resolve the house/$P_5$ open case.
2005.02896.title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)

2402.08418.paper_contribution: Develops tournament analogues of Sidorenko's conjecture: proves asymptotically tight bounds on the maximum number of edges in a tournament anti-Sidorenko digraph, gives novel constructions of tournament Sidorenko digraphs, and fully characterises which orientations of stars are tournament Sidorenko or tournament anti-Sidorenko.
2402.08418.title: Sidorenko's Conjecture (Conjecture 1.2)
2402.08418.paper_title: Variations on Sidorenko's conjecture in tournaments

1809.00919.paper_contribution: Proves that for every forest $H$ there exists $c > 0$ such that every graph $G$ that is both $H$-free and $\bar{H}$-free satisfies $\alpha(G)\omega(G) \geq |G|^c$, establishing the Erdős-Hajnal property for all finitely-defined ideals excluding a forest and its complement.
1809.00919.title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
1809.00919.paper_title: Pure pairs. I. Trees and linear anticomplete pairs

2202.10412.paper_contribution: Proves that if $H$ is a good forest then the disjoint union of $H$ and $P_4$ is also good (polynomially $\chi$-bounded), in particular showing that $2P_4$-free graphs satisfy $\chi(G) \leq \omega(G)^{16}$; more generally proves polynomial $\chi$-boundedness for $\{H_1, H_2\}$-free graphs when every component of $H_1$ is good and $H_2$ is a broom or disjoint union of a good forest and paths.
2202.10412.title: Gyárfás-Sumner Conjecture
2202.10412.paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number VI. Adding a four-vertex path

1907.00351.paper_contribution: Shows that Neumann-Lara's Conjecture is equivalent to the more general statement that every $K_5$-minor-free graph $G$ satisfies $\vec{\chi}(G) \leq 2$ and moreover any orientation of $G$ admits an acyclic 2-colouring without monochromatic triangles.
1907.00351.title: Neumann-Lara / Škrekovski Conjecture (Conjecture 1)
1907.00351.paper_title: A Note on Graphs of Dichromatic Number 2

1808.04757.paper_contribution: Resolves the question: the formula is confirmed for $n \in \{6, 7, 8, 9\}$ (corresponding odd cycles $C_{13}, C_{15}, C_{17}, C_{19}$) but shown to fail for $n = 11$.
1808.04757.title: Question of Watanabe, Ishii, and Sawa on N3 of odd cycles
1808.04757.paper_title: Addressing Johnson graphs, complete multipartite graphs, odd cycles and other graphs

2505.05339.paper_contribution: Disproves the conjecture for $r=3$ by exhibiting the line hypergraph of the Biggs-Smith graph (a cubic edge-transitive graph on 102 vertices) as an explicit counterexample; a second counterexample, the line hypergraph of the graph F168D on 168 vertices, is also identified.
2505.05339.title: Lovász Conjecture (Conjecture 1.2)
2505.05339.paper_title: A Counterexample to a Conjecture of Lovász

2004.07457.paper_contribution: Proves Corollary 10: for any $\varepsilon > 0$ and $\Delta$ large enough, every bipartite graph with maximum degree at most $\Delta$ is $((1+\varepsilon)\Delta/\log^4\Delta,\,2)$- and $((1+\varepsilon)\Delta/\log\Delta,\,\log\Delta)$-choosable, constituting the first asymmetric progress toward the conjecture.
2004.07457.title: Conjecture 2 (Krivelevich–Alon)
2004.07457.paper_title: Asymmetric list sizes in bipartite graphs

2505.07002.paper_contribution: Proves that every 2-edge-connected graph embedded in the torus admits a nowhere-zero 4-flow unless it is Petersen-like (in which case it provably does not); this vastly strengthens the Tutte 4-Flow Conjecture on the torus because almost all toroidal graphs contain a Petersen minor but almost none are Petersen-like.
2505.07002.title: Tutte 4-Flow Conjecture (Conjecture 1)
2505.07002.paper_title: Three-edge-coloring (Tait coloring) cubic graphs on the torus: A proof of Grünbaum's conjecture

1703.08123.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for the special case of digraphs with bounded stability number, using the fact that the fractional domination of vertices by stable sets is at most 2 when the stability number is bounded.
1703.08123.title: Conjecture 3 (General Sands-Sauer-Woodrow Conjecture)
1703.08123.paper_title: A proof of the Erdős-Sands-Sauer-Woodrow conjecture

1807.06285.paper_contribution: Proves an affirmative answer to a strengthening of Bukh's question for the fractional chromatic number: for every graph $G$ with $\chi_f(G) \geq t$, the fractional chromatic number of $G_p$ is at least $t/(8\log_{1/p}(et)+4)$ with probability at least $1-1/(2t)$.
1807.06285.title: Problem 1 (Bukh)
1807.06285.paper_title: Fractional chromatic number of a random subgraph

2409.01513.paper_contribution: Proves that every bipartite graph $G$ of sufficiently large maximum degree $\Delta$ satisfies $\operatorname{ch}(G)<(\frac{4}{5}-\varepsilon)\frac{\Delta}{\log\Delta}$ for $\varepsilon=10^{-3}$, giving the first upper bound with leading constant strictly less than $1$ and showing that list coloring behaves fundamentally differently for bipartite graphs than for general triangle-free graphs.
2409.01513.title: Conjecture 1.1 (Alon–Krivelevich)

2412.21194.paper_contribution: Proves Alon's conjecture for all abelian groups of order $N$ for almost all $N$; additionally, for finite vector spaces of order $N$ with characteristic $\equiv 1\pmod{4}$, proves the existence of a self-complementary Cayley graph with clique number and independence number both at most $(2+o(1))\log N$.
2412.21194.title: Conjecture 1.9 (Alon's Conjecture on Ramsey Cayley Graphs)
2412.21194.paper_title: On the clique number of random Cayley graphs and related topics

2301.10147.paper_contribution: Proves that every $H$-free graph $G$ with $|G|\geq 2$ satisfies $\kappa(G)\geq 2^{c\sqrt{\log|G|}\,\log\log|G|}$ (Theorem 1.3), the first improvement over the $2^{c\sqrt{\log|G|}}$ bound for general $H$ since 1977, obtained as a corollary of a stronger quantitative result (Theorem 1.8) that also strengthens theorems of Rödl, Nikiforov, and Fox–Sudakov.
2301.10147.title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
2301.10147.paper_title: Induced subgraph density. I. A loglog step towards Erdos-Hajnal

2210.16971.paper_contribution: Proves two necessary conditions for the directed forcing property (Theorem 1.6) and shows that an oriented graph $B$ with a homomorphism $B \to \vec{K}_2$ has the directed forcing property if and only if its underlying undirected bipartite graph has the asymmetric forcing property (Theorem 1.8).
2210.16971.title: Forcing Conjecture (Skokan–Thoma)
2210.16971.paper_title: A note on directed analogues of the Sidorenko and forcing conjectures

1803.03588.paper_contribution: Proves that for every $C_5$-free graph $G$ with $n > 1$ vertices, $\max(\alpha(G), \omega(G)) \geq 2^{c\sqrt{\log n \log \log n}}$, improving the previously best known general bound of $2^{c\sqrt{\log n}}$ when $H = C_5$.
1803.03588.title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
1803.03588.paper_title: Towards Erdos-Hajnal for graphs with no 5-hole

2405.16586.paper_contribution: Proves a strengthening of the conjecture for the projective plane: a 2-connected graph embedded in the projective plane admits a nowhere-zero 4-flow if and only if it is not Petersen-like (Theorem 1.6 and Corollary 1.3).
2405.16586.title: Tutte 4-Flow Conjecture (Conjecture 1)
2405.16586.paper_title: Three-edge-coloring projective planar cubic graphs: A generalization of the Four Color Theorem

1804.01060.paper_contribution: Establishes equivalence with Conjecture 2.1 via Rödl's theorem (Lemma 3.1), and verifies the statement for ideals defined by excluding induced subdivisions of a fixed graph in both $G$ and $\bar{G}$.
1804.01060.title: Conjecture 2.2 (coherent reformulation of Erdős-Hajnal)
1804.01060.paper_title: Pure pairs. II. Excluding all subdivisions of a graph

2110.09403.paper_contribution: Disproves this conjecture by showing that the maximum list chromatic number of $K_t$-minor free graphs is at least $(2-o(1))t$, which strictly exceeds $\frac{3}{2}t$ for all sufficiently large $t$.
2110.09403.title: Conjecture 4 (Kawarabayashi-Mohar, $c = \tfrac{3}{2}$)
2110.09403.paper_title: Improved lower bound for the list chromatic number of graphs with no $K_t$ minor

2105.09871.paper_contribution: Proves Tuza's Conjecture for threshold graphs (graphs that are both split graphs and cographs) and for even balanced co-chain graphs (co-chain graphs where both sides have the same size divisible by 4).
2105.09871.title: Tuza's Conjecture

2301.08707.paper_contribution: Confirms the conjecture by proving that every graph on $n$ vertices admits a strongly-separating path system of size $19n$.
2301.08707.attributed_to: Balogh, Csaba, Martin, and Pluhár; Falgas-Ravry, Kittipassorn, Korándi, Letzter, and Narayanan
2301.08707.title: Conjecture 1
2301.08707.paper_title: Separating the edges of a graph by a linear number of paths

2006.11353.paper_contribution: The conjecture is discussed in Section 3 as related context on connections between clique colourings and perfect graphs; no new results concerning it are proved in this paper.
2006.11353.title: Hoang–McDiarmid Conjecture
2006.11353.paper_title: Tight Bounds on the Clique Chromatic Number

1908.03788.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full generality as Theorem 1.9, via a stronger induction hypothesis (Theorem 2.4), superseding all previous partial results.
1908.03788.attributed_to: Beisegel, Chudnovsky, Gurvich, Milanič, and Servatius
1908.03788.title: Beisegel et al. Conjecture 1

2602.11990.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture holds when $H = P_{a,b}$, i.e., the complete bipartite graph $K_{a,b+2}$ together with an additional vertex adjacent to exactly two vertices on the larger side of the bipartition, for all $a,b \geq 1$; as a special case this covers $H = K_4^{++}$, the graph obtained from $K_4$ by subdividing two disjoint edges.
2602.11990.title: Scott's Conjecture
2602.11990.paper_title: A positive instance of Scott's Conjecture on induced subdivisions

1807.11733.paper_contribution: The paper does not directly prove new cases of the Reconstruction Conjecture, but establishes the related result that the number of edges of any graph on $n$ vertices is reconstructible from any deck missing at most $\frac{1}{20}\sqrt{n}$ cards (Theorem 1.2), contributing to the programme of partial-deck reconstruction.
1807.11733.title: Reconstruction Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)

1709.09050.paper_contribution: The survey presents this conjecture alongside Theorem 14, which establishes $c(\lfloor g/2 \rfloor) \leq \tilde{c}(g) \leq \frac{3}{2}(g+1)$.
1709.09050.title: Clarke-Fiorini-Joret-Theis Conjecture
1709.09050.paper_title: Topological directions in Cops and Robbers

2212.09971.paper_contribution: By computing genus polynomials for all cubic graphs with up to 16 vertices, identifies 226 cubic graphs whose genus polynomials have at least one non-real root, and exhibits six specific graphs whose complex roots satisfy $|\Re(z)| < |\Im(z)|/\sqrt{3}$, lying outside the cone guaranteed to yield log-concave quadratic factors.
2212.09971.title: Conjecture 1 (Stahl)
2212.09971.paper_title: Genus Polynomials of Cubic Graphs with Non-Real Roots

2306.02364.paper_contribution: Proves two partial results in its favour: Theorem 1.3 establishes the case where $A$ is a cyclic triangle (and $\chi(B)\geq c$), and Theorem 1.4 establishes the conjecture for all tournaments with sufficiently large domination number.
2306.02364.title: Conjecture 1.2
2306.02364.paper_title: Some results and problems on tournament structure

1905.12749.paper_contribution: Proves Theorem 1.2, an approximate version: $\max_{x \in \mathbb{Z}} \Pr(X_H = x) = n^{1-v(H)+o(1)}$ for every connected graph $H$ and fixed $p \in (0,1)$, matching the conjectured bound up to the $o(1)$ term in the exponent.
1905.12749.title: Conjecture 1.1
1905.12749.paper_title: Anticoncentration for subgraph counts in random graphs

2202.13306.paper_contribution: The paper proves Theorems 5.2 and 5.3 establishing new partial results towards this conjecture in the setting of oriented forests, building on the known reduction to trees.
2202.13306.title: Conjecture 5.1
2202.13306.paper_title: Heroes in oriented complete multipartite graphs

2602.07435.paper_contribution: Proves that every connected graph with vertex cover number $k$ has cop number at most $k/2^{(1-o(1))\sqrt{\log k}}$, giving the first sublinear upper bound on the cop number in terms of the vertex cover number.
2602.07435.title: Meyniel's Conjecture
2602.07435.paper_title: Cops and robber in graphs with bounded vertex cover number

2204.00722.paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 2 for all hereditary subclasses of interval graphs and rooted directed path graphs by establishing that these classes are effectively delineated by twin-width; also shows Conjecture 2 cannot be settled this way for directed path graphs (multiple roots), axis-parallel two-lengthed segment graphs, and visibility graphs of simple polygons.
2204.00722.title: Conjecture 2

2108.07066.paper_contribution: Proves that for every double star $H$ the bounding function $f$ in the Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture can be chosen to be a polynomial, i.e., $\chi(G) \leq f(\omega(G))$ with $f$ a polynomial for every $H$-free graph $G$.
2108.07066.title: Gyárfás-Sumner Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
2108.07066.paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number. III. Excluding a double star

2604.13700.paper_contribution: The paper does not directly resolve Problem 1 for the remaining open cases, but proves the related Mader conjecture that $c_r\to\infty$, showing regular digraphs are fundamentally better behaved than general digraphs.
2604.13700.title: Problem 1 (Seymour's Problem)
2604.13700.paper_title: Openly disjoint cycles and directed tree-width of regular digraphs

2605.01223.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for the special case of hereditary classes defined by finitely many excluded induced subgraphs, as a corollary of the main result Theorem 1.4.
2605.01223.title: Conjecture 1.1
2605.01223.paper_title: Tree-alpha and excluding finitely many graphs

2107.11780.paper_contribution: Proves Esperet's conjecture for all star-forests $H$ (forests in which every component is a star), via the main theorem: if $H'$ satisfies the conjecture and $H$ is the disjoint union of $H'$ and a star, then $H$ also satisfies the conjecture.
2107.11780.title: Esperet's Conjecture (Conjecture 1.2)
2107.11780.paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number. II. Excluding a star-forest

2112.02313.paper_contribution: Extends the treewidth confirmation of Cereceda's conjecture (due to Bonamy and Bousquet) to the setting of non-trivial Kempe changes with one fewer color: proves any two $k$-colorings of an $n$-vertex graph of treewidth $\mathrm{tw}$ are equivalent up to $O(\mathrm{tw}\cdot n^2)$ Kempe changes when $k > \mathrm{tw} + 1$ (Theorem 0.3).
2112.02313.title: Cereceda's Conjecture

2402.05753.paper_contribution: No direct proof of Conjecture 1.3 for graphs; however, because Conjecture 1.5 (for geodesic surfaces) implies Conjecture 1.3, and this paper settles Conjecture 1.5 for constant-curvature (hyperbolic) surfaces of arbitrary genus by proving $c(M)=2$, it confirms the graph analogue for the special family of graphs embeddable in such surfaces.
2402.05753.title: Conjecture 1.3
2402.05753.paper_title: Cops and Robber on Hyperbolic Manifolds

2006.09877.paper_contribution: Proves that every graph class of bounded twin-width admits an $O(\log n)$-adjacency labeling scheme, confirming the implicit graph conjecture for all bounded twin-width hereditary classes.
2006.09877.title: Implicit Graph Conjecture

1802.02283.paper_contribution: This paper (the second in a two-paper series) gives a polynomial-time algorithm that converts any 4-precoloring of a $P_6$-free graph into a polynomial-size collection of excellent starred precolorings; combined with the companion paper's algorithm for excellent precolorings, the two papers together prove Huang's conjecture in full.
1802.02283.title: Huang's Conjecture
1802.02283.paper_title: Four-coloring $P_6$-free graphs. II. Finding an excellent precoloring

2005.05042.paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 1.1 in full, establishing as Theorem 1.2 that every (theta, pyramid, prism, turtle)-free graph $G$ has at most $|V(G)|^{18}$ minimal separators, constructible in polynomial time.
2005.05042.title: Conjecture 1.1
2005.05042.paper_title: Graphs with polynomially many minimal separators

2012.05554.paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 4 for all minor-closed classes with bounded pathwidth by establishing $\chi^\star(\mathcal{G}) \leq 2\,\mathrm{tcn}(\mathcal{G}) - 2$ in that setting (Theorem 2), and shows this bound is best possible; the general conjecture remains open.
2012.05554.title: Conjecture 4
2012.05554.paper_title: Clustered colouring of graph classes with bounded treedepth or pathwidth

2605.10112.paper_contribution: Proves that every 5-chromatic graph contains a dominating $K_{5}$-model $(T_1,\dots,T_5)$ with $T_4=\{v\}$ and $T_5=\{w\}$ singletons and $v,w$ adjacent, providing structural progress strictly stronger than the known $K_5$-minor result and contributing towards the existence of a $K_5$-subdivision in every 5-chromatic graph.
2605.10112.title: Hajós' Conjecture (k=5)

1912.03236.paper_contribution: Proves analogues for $\ell$-connected graphs: the exact tight bound $P_G(k) \leq (k-1)!((k-1)^{n-k+1}+(-1)^{n-k})$ for $2$-connected graphs (with equality iff $G \cong G_{n,k}$), and the asymptotically tight bound $P_G(k) \leq k!(k-1)^{n-\ell-k+1}+O((k-2)^n)$ for $\ell \geq 3$.
1912.03236.title: Tomescu's Graph Coloring Conjecture
1912.03236.paper_title: Tomescu's graph coloring conjecture for $\ell$-connected graphs

2507.06169.paper_contribution: Provides a second, simpler counterexample to Conjecture 1.5 that additionally has arbitrarily large girth and does not contain all trees as induced subgraphs; the proof establishes a slightly stronger property than (v) — see Theorem 6.2.
2507.06169.title: Conjecture 1.5

2309.04390.paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 1.3 in full; more precisely, proves two stronger results: (a) for every $K_4$-free chordal graph $H$, every (even hole, $K_4$)-free graph of sufficiently large treewidth contains an induced copy of $H$ (the diamond case yields the conjecture), and (b) for every forest $H$ and every $t\in\mathbb{N}$, every even-hole-free graph of sufficiently large treewidth contains either $K_t$ or an induced subgraph obtained from $H$ by adding a universal vertex.
2309.04390.title: Conjecture 1.3
2309.04390.paper_title: Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions XI. Local structure in even-hole-free graphs of large treewidth

2211.01032.paper_contribution: Proves (Theorem 1.8) that $\mathbb{E}[F(K_n)] = \Theta(\ln n)$, confirming the logarithmic order; the exact constant $2\ln n + O(1)$ remains open.
2211.01032.title: Conjecture 9.4
2211.01032.paper_title: Random Embeddings of Graphs: The Expected Number of Faces in Most Graphs is Logarithmic

2004.12166.paper_contribution: Proves that a constructive version of the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture implies the improved approximation conjecture (Conjecture 5), and shows the improved approximation property is closed under graph substitution, establishing Conjecture 5 for many graphs $H$ (e.g., $P_6$-free, fork-free) where the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture remains open.
2004.12166.title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 3)
2004.12166.paper_title: An algorithmic weakening of the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture

1903.05363.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture holds for every $c \leq 12$ (Theorem 1.1) and disproves it for every $c \geq 13$ via explicit constructions of 3-connected $c$-crossing-critical graphs containing arbitrarily many vertices of arbitrarily large degree (Theorem 1.2, Corollary 1.3).
1903.05363.title: Richter's Bounded Maximum Degree Conjecture
1903.05363.paper_title: Bounded maximum degree conjecture holds precisely for $c$-crossing-critical graphs with $c \leq 12$

2012.05287.paper_contribution: Answers the question affirmatively: proves that every non-null even-hole-free claw-free graph has a simplicial clique, and establishes the stronger result (Theorem 2.2) that every non-null clean graph (claw-free and containing no jewel, line wheel, short prism, or seven-antihole) has a simplicial clique.
2012.05287.title: Question 1.1

2305.13422.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for $l \geq C_\varepsilon k^3 (\log k)^{1+\varepsilon}$ (Theorem 1.3) and establishes the general upper bound $f(l,k) \leq D_\varepsilon k(\log k)^{1+\varepsilon} \cdot l^{k-1}$ (Theorem 1.5), reducing the gap from exponential to polynomial in $k$.
2305.13422.title: Conjecture 1.2 (Lefmann, Rödl, Thomas)

1510.06964.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for all $k \geq 4$: if $G$ is a connected non-complete $k$-regular graph with $k \geq 4$, then the set of $k$-colourings of $G$ is a Kempe class (Theorem 1.1).
1510.06964.title: Mohar's Conjecture
1510.06964.paper_title: On a conjecture of Mohar concerning Kempe equivalence of regular graphs

1705.00089.paper_contribution: Proves $\tau(\mathcal{F}) \leq c\nu(\mathcal{F})$ for special families of axis-parallel rectangles in $\mathbb{R}^2$ (e.g., $r$-bounded aspect ratio in Theorem 1.10, corner intersections with a maximum matching in Corollary 1.9), establishing a constant ratio in these special cases but not the specific bound of $2$ in general.
1705.00089.title: Wegner's Conjecture

2202.11641.paper_contribution: Provides a matching-theoretic translation showing that Conjecture 1.1 holds if and only if all cubic, 3-connected, Pfaffian, bipartite graphs are Hamiltonian (Theorem 1.3), and reduces the conjecture to cubic, planar braces (Lemma 1.2), also proving analogous reductions for four stronger Hamiltonicity properties in cubic, 3-connected, bipartite graphs without requiring planarity.
2202.11641.title: Barnette's Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)

2312.17130.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for all Schrijver graphs and Kneser graphs for any choice of parameters; also proves that every graph $G$ with $\mathrm{zig}(G)\geq t$ contains $K_{\lfloor t/2\rfloor+1}$ as an odd-minor, showing topological lower bounds cannot yield counterexamples.
2312.17130.title: Odd Hadwiger's Conjecture

2101.04590.paper_contribution: Proves $\mathrm{sm}_{\tilde{\chi}}(t) \leq 2m_\chi(t) - 1$ and $\mathrm{bm}_{\tilde{\chi}}(t) \leq 2m_\chi(2t) - 1 = O(t(\log\log t)^6)$, reducing the asymptotics of these dichromatic forcing functions to the undirected Hadwiger problem and obtaining almost-linear bounds by combining with Postle's result $m_\chi(t) = O(t(\log\log t)^6)$.
2101.04590.title: Hadwiger's Conjecture
2101.04590.paper_title: Complete minors in digraphs with given dichromatic number

1712.06067.paper_contribution: Proves the full conjecture for all $k \geq 4$ and establishes the equality characterisation, completing the proof after Knox and Mohar had previously settled the cases $k=4$ and $k=5$.
1712.06067.title: Tomescu's Conjecture
1712.06067.paper_title: A proof of Tomescu's graph coloring conjecture

1708.05424.paper_contribution: Reproves the forward direction (bounded expansion implies $O(n^\varepsilon)$-dimension for fixed height) via weak coloring numbers with a simpler proof and quantitatively better bounds (Theorem 2); explicitly notes the backward direction remains open.
1708.05424.title: Conjecture 4
1708.05424.paper_title: Nowhere Dense Graph Classes and Dimension

2403.17568.paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 3 in full generality as a corollary of Theorem 4, which shows every graph $G$ (with no minimum-degree restriction) has an induced linear forest with at least $f(G)$ vertices where $f(d)=1$ for $d=0$, $f(d)=5/6$ for $d=1$, and $f(d)=2/(d+1)$ for $d\geqslant 2$.
2403.17568.attributed_to: Akbari, Amanihamedani, Mousavi, Nikpey, and Sheybani
2403.17568.title: Conjecture 3 (Akbari et al.)
2403.17568.paper_title: A Caro-Wei bound for induced linear forests in graphs

2508.08870.paper_contribution: Proves a weaker linear lower bound: any $d$-dimensional set of $n$ points determines at least $bdn$ lines with pairwise distinct directions for an absolute constant $b \geq \frac{1}{48}$, providing the first result linear in $dn$.
2508.08870.title: Conjecture 1.1
2508.08870.paper_title: Distinct Directions and Distinct Distances in $\mathbb{R}^d$

1812.07327.paper_contribution: Disproves the conjecture by establishing Corollary 3: for every positive integer $c$ there exists a graph with fractional chromatic number at least $c$ and Hall ratio at most $18$.
1812.07327.title: Harris' Conjecture
1812.07327.paper_title: 1-subdivisions, fractional chromatic number and Hall ratio

2002.11100.paper_contribution: Proves that any $K_s$-free $n$-vertex graph with $\alpha(G) \leq r$ has a clique minor of order at least $(n/r)^{1+\frac{1}{10(s-2)}}$ (Theorem 1), which substantially exceeds the bound $n/\alpha(G)$ that Hadwiger's conjecture would imply for those graphs.
2002.11100.title: Hadwiger's Conjecture
2002.11100.paper_title: Clique minors in graphs with a forbidden subgraph

2407.18346.paper_contribution: Settles the conjecture affirmatively by constructing explicit graphs of arbitrarily large girth that admit no KT orientation.
2407.18346.title: Sadhukhan's Conjecture
2407.18346.paper_title: Orientations of graphs with at most one directed path between every pair of vertices

2209.09107.paper_contribution: The paper's Combinatorial Nullstellensatz-based sufficient condition (Theorem 1.6) applies to both conjectures and yields new partial results toward each.
2209.09107.attributed_to: Akbari, Dalirrooyfard, Ehsani, Ozeki, and Sherkati
2209.09107.title: Conjecture 1.2

2011.08049.paper_contribution: States the conjecture as background motivation for focusing on the dense-graph regime; the paper does not prove or disprove it.
2011.08049.title: Conjecture 1.1
2011.08049.paper_title: Efficient polynomial-time approximation scheme for the genus of dense graphs

1510.00549.paper_contribution: Proves that every $\left(\lfloor n/2\rfloor - 2\right)$-bishellable drawing $D$ of $K_n$ satisfies $\mathrm{cr}(D) \geq H(n)$, strictly enlarging the class of drawings covered beyond the previously known shellable drawings (Theorem 4), and exhibits an infinite family of drawings that are bishellable but not shellable (Theorem 5).
1510.00549.title: Harary–Hill Conjecture

2403.16319.paper_contribution: Builds on the refutation by Ellis, Ivan and Leader [4], who showed $L(d)\leq\frac{5}{6}\binom{d}{\lfloor d/2\rfloor}$ for $d\geq 4$; the paper analyzes their construction further and obtains improved bounds via Turán density arguments for hypergraph augmentations.
2403.16319.title: Conjecture on L(d)
2403.16319.paper_title: Erasure codes and Turán hypercube problems

2009.03418.paper_contribution: Provides the first very general construction of drawings of $K_n$ attaining exactly $H(n)$ crossings via antipodal geodesic drawings on the unit sphere $S^2$, gives a 'book proof' that each such drawing achieves $H(n)$, and derives as a corollary that $\mathrm{cr}_{S^2}(K_n) \leq H(n)$; also gives a simple explanation of Moon's 1968 asymptotic result that random geodesic drawings approach $H(n)$.
2009.03418.title: Hill Conjecture
2009.03418.paper_title: On a conjecture by Anthony Hill

2103.04191.paper_contribution: The paper studies directed extensions of this conjecture (Conjectures 1.4 and 1.5) and proves several special cases, explicitly framed as resolving special cases of an extension of the Gyárfás-Sumner conjecture to directed graphs.
2103.04191.title: Gyárfás-Sumner Conjecture
2103.04191.paper_title: On coloring digraphs with forbidden induced subgraphs

2310.04265.paper_contribution: The paper introduces Conjecture 5.3 (large dom implies an $\operatorname{\overrightarrow{\omega}}$-cluster) as an intermediate conjecture between Theorem 5.1 and Conjecture 5.2, and establishes implications among the three statements.
2310.04265.title: Large dom implies a dom-cluster Conjecture

2206.12335.paper_contribution: Proves $p_{\mathrm{giant}}\leq 0.5847$, improving the previously known upper bound; the conjecture that $p_{\mathrm{giant}}=1/2$ remains open.
2206.12335.title: Falgas-Ravry–Pfenninger Conjecture on $p_{\mathrm{giant}}$

1603.00197.paper_contribution: Proves the full statement of the conjecture for every tree $T$, completely settling it in the affirmative.
1603.00197.title: Barát-Thomassen Conjecture
1603.00197.paper_title: A Proof of the Barát-Thomassen Conjecture

2111.07147.paper_contribution: Theorem 5(ii) answers this question positively, showing that the class of triangle-free graphs drawn on any fixed surface has weak diameter choosability at most two (which implies clustered choosability at most two for bounded-degree graphs).
2111.07147.title: Question 3 (Esperet and Joret)
2111.07147.paper_title: Weak diameter coloring of graphs on surfaces

2510.11311.paper_contribution: Shows that the most direct extension of Thomassen's conjecture to digraphs (using minimum out-degree) is false; proves all orientations of $C_3$ and $C_5$ are avoidable; and fully characterizes which digraphs are avoidable when restricted to regular host digraphs.
2510.11311.title: Thomassen's Conjecture
2510.11311.paper_title: Extending Thomassen's conjecture to directed graphs

2201.08204.paper_contribution: Disproves Conjecture 1.1 for all $r \geq 5$ by constructing, for every $n$, a graph $G$ with $\chi(G) \geq n$ and $\omega(G) \leq 3$ such that every induced subgraph $H$ with $\omega(H) \leq 2$ satisfies $\chi(H) \leq 4$; the case $r = 4$ remains open.
2201.08204.title: Conjecture 1.1
2201.08204.paper_title: A counterexample to a conjecture about triangle-free induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number

1803.03704.paper_contribution: The paper disproves Conjecture 3 for trees of maximum degree three by constructing a counterexample based on complete binary trees $T_k$ of arbitrary depth $k$, showing that no function $f$ can make the conjecture hold in that case.
1803.03704.title: Conjecture 3

1809.04716.paper_contribution: Proves that for any $\Delta$-regular graph $G$, $la(G) \leq \frac{\Delta}{2} + C\Delta^{2/3-\alpha}$ for absolute constants $\alpha > 0$ and $C > 0$, improving the Alon–Spencer bound $\frac{\Delta}{2} + O(\Delta^{2/3}(\log\Delta)^{1/3})$ by a polynomial factor; also gives better bounds for graphs that are sufficiently good spectral expanders.
1809.04716.title: Linear Arboricity Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)

1010.2472.paper_contribution: The paper does not make direct progress on Steinberg's Conjecture; it is cited only as a related open problem in the context of planar graph 3-colorability.
1010.2472.title: Steinberg's Conjecture
1010.2472.paper_title: Three-coloring triangle-free graphs on surfaces I. Extending a coloring to a disk with one triangle

2109.01310.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full, as a special case of the more general Theorem 1.5: every (theta, pyramid, generalized prism, $K_t$)-free graph $G$ satisfies $\mathrm{tw}(G) \leq c_t \log(|V(G)|)$ for a constant $c_t$ depending only on $t$.
2109.01310.title: Conjecture 1.4 (Sintiari and Trotignon)
2109.01310.paper_title: Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions III. Three-path-configurations and logarithmic treewidth

1606.06114.paper_contribution: Proves a relaxed version: every planar digraph of digirth at least 4 is 2-colourable (the previously known result required digirth at least 5).
1606.06114.title: Conjecture 1.1 (Neumann-Lara)
1606.06114.paper_title: Planar digraphs of digirth four are 2-colourable

2202.07746.paper_contribution: Proved as a corollary of Theorem 3: $E[F] \leq n(H_{2\mu}+1)$, and since $H_{2\mu} \sim \log(2\mu)+\gamma$, this gives $O(n\log\mu)$; the bound is tight up to a constant factor.
2202.07746.attributed_to: Halasz, Masarik, Samal, Campion Loth, and Mohar
2202.07746.title: Conjecture 2
2202.07746.paper_title: Expected number of faces in a random embedding of any graph is at most linear

1710.11281.paper_contribution: Establishes the lower bound $c(g) \geq g^{1/2 - o(1)}$ using random-graph results of Bollobás, Kun, and Leader, and proposes the stronger Conjecture 8 that $c(g) = g^{1/2+o(1)}$, suggesting Schroeder's linear upper bound is far from tight.
1710.11281.title: Schroeder's Conjecture (Conjecture 2)
1710.11281.paper_title: Notes on Cops and Robber game on graphs

2112.02378.paper_contribution: Proves that every $r$-quasiplanar graph with $n \geq r$ vertices, for $r = 2s$ and $s \geq 3$, has at most $n\bigl(\frac{c\log n}{s}\bigr)^{2s-4}$ edges, where $c > 0$ is an absolute constant, improving all previously known upper bounds.
2112.02378.attributed_to: Combinatorial geometry community; see Brass, Moser, and Pach [BrMP05]
2112.02378.title: Conjecture 1.1 (r-Quasiplanar Graph Conjecture)
2112.02378.paper_title: Quasiplanar Graphs, String Graphs, and the Erdos-Gallai Problem

2603.17630.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in a strong form: for every connected graph $G$ with $n$ vertices and minimum degree at least $d$, and every tree $T$, $\mathbb{P}(\mathcal{T}\cong T)\leq n^{-\Omega(d)}$, with the exponent growing linearly in $d$.
2603.17630.title: Conjecture 1.3 (Lee)
2603.17630.paper_title: Anticoncentration of random spanning trees in graphs with large minimum degree

2005.10849.paper_contribution: Uses this conjecture conditionally to support the claim that the exponent $\frac{1}{4}$ in their lower bound is best possible; does not prove the conjecture itself.
2005.10849.attributed_to: Folklore (Bollobás and Szemerédi for the cubic case)
2005.10849.title: Conjecture 1.3 (Folklore)
2005.10849.paper_title: On the cop number of graphs of high girth

2206.00594.paper_contribution: Theorem 1.1 of this paper (logarithmic feedback vertex set for sparse $\mathcal{O}_k$-free graphs) was the key tool used by Nguyen, Scott, and Seymour [NSS24] to resolve this conjecture in the more general $\mathcal{O}_k$-free case.
2206.00594.title: Conjecture 1.5 (Le)
2206.00594.paper_title: Sparse graphs with bounded induced cycle packing number have logarithmic treewidth

2003.01846.paper_contribution: Provides several new infinite families of minimal non-strongly-perfect graphs (emanations and mutations of Graphs II–VI) that are not among Graphs I–V of the conjecture, demonstrating that the forbidden induced subgraph list in Conjecture 1.1 is incomplete.
2003.01846.title: Conjecture 1.1
2003.01846.paper_title: New Examples of Minimal Non-Strongly-Perfect Graphs

2307.15032.paper_contribution: Proves that for every path $P$, every $P$-free graph on $n$ vertices contains a clique or stable set of size at least $2^{(\log n)^{1-o(1)}}$, establishing the 'near Erdős-Hajnal' property for all paths and strictly improving the previously known $2^{c\sqrt{\log n \log\log n}}$ bound in this case.
2307.15032.title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1 / 1.2)
2307.15032.paper_title: Induced subgraph density. V. All paths approach Erdos-Hajnal

1904.12060.paper_contribution: Cited as foundational background motivation; the paper's main result (proving the list total coloring analogue for planar graphs with $\Delta \geq 8$) is consistent with this conjecture but does not directly prove new cases of it.
1904.12060.title: Conjecture 1 (List Coloring Conjectures)
1904.12060.paper_title: Every planar graph with $Δ\geqslant 8$ is totally $(Δ+2)$-choosable

1812.02420.paper_contribution: Introduces the notion of circular homomorphisms between digraphs as the natural framework in which this conjecture is formulated, and develops the theory of circular $F$-colourability (Section 2) that underpins the conjecture.
1812.02420.title: Conjecture 5.39
1812.02420.paper_title: On the Complexity of Digraph Colourings and Vertex Arboricity

2406.01068.paper_contribution: Proves that $c_2(G) \leq 3$ for every planar graph $G$, confirming Yang's conjecture and simultaneously strengthening the classical theorem of Aigner and Fromme that $c(G) \leq 3$ for planar graphs.
2406.01068.title: Yang's Conjecture
2406.01068.paper_title: Guarding isometric subgraphs and Cops and Robber in planar graphs
paper_authors: Sebastián González Hermosillo de la Maza

2007.14944.paper_contribution: Proves a local asymptotic version: for graphs with sufficiently large maximum degree $\Delta$ and minimum degree $\delta \geq \ln^{25} \Delta$, there is an $L$-edge-colouring whenever $|L(e)| \geq (1+\varepsilon)\max\{\deg(u),\deg(v)\}$ for each edge $e = uv$; also extends this to weighted lists, correspondence colouring, and linear $k$-uniform hypergraphs.
2007.14944.title: List (Edge) Colouring Conjecture
2007.14944.paper_title: Edge-colouring graphs with local list sizes

2202.05557.paper_contribution: Proves that for every tree $H$ of radius two and every integer $d \geq 1$, there is a polynomial $f$ such that $\chi(G) \leq f(\tau_d(G))$ for every $H$-free graph $G$ (Theorem 1.6), which in particular yields a doubly-exponential bound on $\chi(G)$ in terms of $\omega(G)$ for $H$-free graphs when $H$ is a tree of radius two.
2202.05557.title: Conjecture 1.1 (Gyárfás-Sumner Conjecture)
2202.05557.paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number. V. Excluding a tree of radius two and a complete multipartite graph

2307.15512.paper_contribution: Proves that whp the cop number of the $k$-uniform binomial random hypergraph $G^k(n,p)$ is $\tilde{O}\!\left(\sqrt{n/k}\right)$ for a broad range of $k$ and $p$ (Corollary 1.8), establishing the hypergraph analogue of Meyniel's conjecture in the random setting.
2307.15512.title: Meyniel's Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
2307.15512.paper_title: Catching a robber on a random $k$-uniform hypergraph

2409.18220.paper_contribution: Proves the first known linear lower bound $s(G) \geq \frac{3n}{4}$ for every connected graph $G$ of order $n \geq 4$, via a superadditivity result for positive and negative square energies over vertex-induced subgraphs.
2409.18220.title: Conjecture 1 (Elphick, Farber, Goldberg, Wocjan)
2409.18220.paper_title: A Linear Lower Bound for the Square Energy of Graphs

1808.01605.paper_contribution: Proves the fractional chromatic number analogue of Rödl's $g=4$ case (Theorem 3): for every real $x \geq 1$ there exists $k(x)$ such that every graph $G$ with $\chi_f(G) \geq k(x)$ contains a triangle-free subgraph $H$ with $\chi_f(H) \geq x$. Also verifies the conjecture for Kneser graphs.
1808.01605.title: Erdős–Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1)
1808.01605.paper_title: Triangle-free subgraphs with large fractional chromatic number

2312.01028.paper_contribution: Theorem 1.1 establishes the homogeneous regularity property (equivalently the mighty Erdős–Hajnal property) for the hereditary family of intersection graphs of pseudo-segments; Lemma 2.13 then yields the polynomial Rödl property for this family as a consequence.
2312.01028.title: Fox–Sudakov Polynomial Rödl Conjecture
2312.01028.paper_title: A structure theorem for pseudo-segments and its applications

2209.00594.paper_contribution: Proves the $t = 5$ case as a corollary (Corollary 1.4) of establishing Holroyd's Strong Hadwiger's conjecture for $t = 4$; additionally shows that every 5-chromatic graph contains a $K_5$-minor with a singleton branch-set, and that in a 5-vertex-critical graph any specified vertex may serve as that singleton branch-set.
2209.00594.title: Hadwiger's Conjecture
2209.00594.paper_title: Strengthening Hadwiger's conjecture for $4$- and $5$-chromatic graphs

1611.03648.paper_contribution: Proves the weaker bound that any $3n-2$ matchings of size $n$ in any graph have a rainbow matching of size $n$ (Theorem 1.9).
1611.03648.title: Conjecture 1.5
1611.03648.paper_title: Large rainbow matchings in general graphs

2301.13175.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full for $t = 5$: every connected $P_5$-free graph has cop number at most two.
2301.13175.title: Sivaraman's Conjecture

2008.09692.paper_contribution: Shows that the authors' Conjecture 11 (on universal cell 3-colorability without $K^+_{3,n-3}$ subcontractions) implies Tutte's 3-Flow Conjecture; establishes Conjecture 11 for subcubic and $K_{3,3}$-minor-free graphs, giving new special-case confirmations of Tutte's conjecture.
2008.09692.title: Tutte's 3-Flow Conjecture

2104.07927.paper_contribution: Proves that for every tree $H$ there exists $c>0$ such that every $H$-free graph $G$ with no $K_{t,t}$ subgraph satisfies $\partial(G)\leq(|H|\zeta t)^c$, establishing polynomial bounds for the $\tau(G)$-analogue of the conjecture and giving a further strengthening of earlier results of Rödl and of Kierstead–Penrice.
2104.07927.title: Gyárfás-Sumner Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
2104.07927.paper_title: Polynomial bounds for chromatic number. I. Excluding a biclique and an induced tree

1701.06301.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture as an immediate corollary of Theorem 1.2 (the $\chi$-boundedness result for classes with no $c+1$ pairwise anticomplete odd holes).
1701.06301.title: Gyárfás Conjecture 6.8
1701.06301.paper_title: Induced subgraphs of graphs with large chromatic number. VII. Gyárfás' complementation conjecture

1912.07203.paper_contribution: Proves improved cop number upper bounds for graphs of bounded diameter: $c(G) < n^{3/5+o(1)}$ when the diameter is at most 4 (improving the prior bound of $n^{2/3+o(1)}$) and $c(G) < n^{3/7+o(1)}$ when the diameter is at most 3 (improving the prior bound of $n^{3/5+o(1)}$), using a novel 'real and imaginary cops' strategy.
1912.07203.title: Meyniel's Conjecture
1912.07203.paper_title: Cops and Robbers on graphs of bounded diameter

1912.06957.paper_contribution: Proves that Meyniel's conjecture for subcubic graphs implies the Weak Meyniel's Conjecture with exponent 3/4 for all graphs (Lemma 7 and Corollary 8); also constructs arbitrarily large subcubic graphs with cop number at least $n^{1/2-o(1)}$ (Theorem 5), strengthening the known lower bounds for bounded-degree graphs.
1912.06957.title: Meyniel's Conjecture
1912.06957.paper_title: Meyniel's conjecture on graphs of bounded degree
paper_authors: Sebastian Gonzalez Hermosillo de la Maza

1704.07219.paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture in full as Theorem 1.3 (every hero is a superhero), thereby completely characterizing all superheroes.
1704.07219.title: Aboulker–Charbit–Naserasr Conjecture (every hero is a superhero)

2206.00186.paper_contribution: Proves that every graph $G$ with $\alpha(G) \leq 2$ contains a minor $H$ with $|V(H)| = \lceil|V(G)|/2\rceil$ and at least $(\gamma - o(1))\binom{|V(H)|}{2}$ edges where $\gamma = 0.986882\ldots$, establishing a dense minor of the exact size predicted by the conjecture for this case (fewer than $1/76$ of all possible edges missing), as progress toward the complete minor.
2206.00186.title: Hadwiger's Conjecture
2206.00186.paper_title: Dense minors of graphs with independence number two

2309.07905.paper_contribution: Proves the $d = 2$ case for graphs with bounded maximum degree (Conjecture 1.4, as Theorem 1.5) and for graphs excluding a topological $K_r$-minor (Theorem 1.9), by showing that $2\chi'_s(G)k$ pairwise disjoint $X$-$Y$-paths guarantee the existence of $k$ pairwise non-adjacent $X$-$Y$-paths.
2309.07905.attributed_to: Georgakopoulos and Papasoglu; Albrechtsen et al.
2309.07905.title: Conjecture 1.2
2309.07905.paper_title: On an induced version of Menger's theorem

2107.05995.paper_contribution: Disproves the conjecture; the planar graph constructed has $\mathrm{HG}(G)=12$ while planar graphs exclude $K_5$ as a minor, giving Hadwiger number at most $4$.
2107.05995.attributed_to: Bosek, Dudek, Farnik, Grytczuk, and Mazur
2107.05995.title: Conjecture on Hat Guessing Number vs. Hadwiger Number (Bosek et al. [4])
2107.05995.paper_title: On the Hat Guessing Number of Graphs

2510.01791.paper_contribution: The paper does not directly prove or disprove Conjecture 1.2; it cites it as motivation for the general Conjecture 1.3, which the paper refutes for all sufficiently large $k$, showing that $\ell_k \sim \frac{k}{2}$ rather than $\ell_k = k$.
2510.01791.attributed_to: Bogdanov, Neustroeva, Sokolov, Volostnov, Russkin, and Voronov
2510.01791.title: Conjecture 1.2
2510.01791.paper_title: On cuts of small chromatic number in sparse graphs
